title: 10 best herbal teas for jaundice

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice symptoms like yellowing of the skin.

Choleretic and Hepatoprotective Effects: Maidenhair Tree tea has been shown to increase bile production and improve liver function, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms like pale stools and dark urine caused by impaired bile flow.

Immunomodulatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba's immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the immune response in cases of jaundice, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with liver damage or disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant properties. It has been shown to improve liver function, which in turn helps to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba tea have anti-inflammatory effects that aid in soothing liver inflammation, allowing the liver to function more efficiently. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support liver health and alleviate symptoms of jaundice in some individuals.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds in Ginkgo biloba tea may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, potentially alleviating jaundice symptoms caused by liver damage.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, which might help protect the liver from damage and improve liver function, contributing to a reduction in jaundice severity.

Quercetin: As a potent antioxidant and flavonoid, quercetin in Ginkgo biloba tea may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, potentially reducing jaundice symptoms associated with liver dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the liver and improve overall health.
Seeds: Used for its potential to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with liver disease.
Barks: Used for its potential to improve liver function and reduce inflammation, although this is not a commonly referenced part in traditional Ginkgo biloba remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose leaves.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired and stir the mixture well to combine.
Serve the herbal ginkgo biloba tea immediately and drink it while still warm for best flavor.

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.
Antioxidant activity: Turmeric tea's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can accumulate in the body and contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate jaundice symptoms.

Bile flow stimulant: Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, helping to improve liver function and alleviate jaundice symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, teas helps with jaundice because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin. Curcumin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and promote the removal of bilirubin, a pigment responsible for jaundice. By reducing liver inflammation and promoting the breakdown of bilirubin, Curcuma longa teas can help to alleviate jaundice symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and promote overall liver health. It also helps to stimulate liver function.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with jaundice by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, thus promoting its normal functioning and bilirubin clearance.
Demethoxycurcumin: It exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the protection of liver cells and the mitigation of jaundice symptoms.
Turmerone (α-Turmerone): It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help in reducing liver inflammation and improving the overall condition of patients with jaundice.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part due to their high content of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa also contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing liver inflammation and promoting liver health for jaundice treatment.
Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa contain various flavonoids and alkaloids, which may aid in detoxifying the liver and reducing bilirubin levels in individuals with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Select high-quality fresh or dried curcuma longa rhizomes for optimal flavor and potency.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried curcuma longa rhizomes or 3-4 fresh pieces into a fine powder.
Steep the ground curcuma longa in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion tea stimulates the production of bile, which helps to remove bilirubin from the bloodstream and reduce jaundice symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with liver damage, which can contribute to jaundice.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with jaundice because it supports liver function and promotes the removal of bilirubin from the blood. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that feeds beneficial gut bacteria, which in turn aids in detoxification. Additionally, dandelion tea's high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, facilitating the body's natural processes to clear jaundice-causing substances.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale tea have been found to exhibit hepatoprotective properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, present in Taraxacum officinale tea, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with jaundice.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 30 grams of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Dry the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Crush the dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the taraxacum officinale powder in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea and discard the solids before serving the tea at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum leaves and flowers in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 2-3 teaspoons of dried silybum marianum leaves and flowers into a fine powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the silybum marianum powder in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to infuse.
Strain the silybum marianum tea immediately and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal tea.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried urtica dioica leaves or 4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.

Steep the urtica dioica leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release the active ingredients.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm contain a high concentration of essential oils that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melissa officinalis leaves or purchase dried form of the herb in amounts sufficient for preparation.
Measure and combine one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves with eight ounces of boiling water for every serving.
Steep the herbal mixture for seven to ten minutes in a cup or teapot to allow flavors and beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solids before consumption.
Drink one to three servings of melissa officinalis tea per day, as needed, for symptom relief from jaundice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with jaundice because it possesses antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the liver. The tea's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce bilirubin levels by stimulating the elimination of waste products from the body. Additionally, St John's wort teas may also help regulate bile flow and improve liver function, ultimately leading to a decrease in jaundice symptoms and a faster recovery time for those affected.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: St John's Wort tea contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, potentially alleviating symptoms of jaundice.

Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a bioactive compound found in St John's Wort, has been shown to have potent antioxidant and free radical scavenging effects, which may help protect the liver from damage and improve bilirubin metabolism, leading to reduced jaundice symptoms.

Choleretic properties: The tea also exhibits choleretic (bile-stimulating) properties, which can help increase bile flow and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, thus alleviating jaundice symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and hypericin, which help reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Leaves: Contain a high concentration of tannins, which have been shown to improve liver function and reduce inflammation associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves from a trusted source in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight for several days.
Combine one teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum with one cup of boiling water to make a strong infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and cooling to drinking temperature.
Drink the tea twice daily, ideally at morning and evening, for up to four weeks under medical supervision.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower teas helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms. The flowers contain flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and improve liver function. When consumed as a tea, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream, which can help clear bilirubin from the body, a common issue in jaundiced individuals. As the toxins are eliminated, the skin's yellowish discoloration may also subside, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Cornflower tea increases bile production and flow, which helps to eliminate bilirubin from the body, reducing the yellowing of skin and eyes associated with jaundice.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in cornflower tea help protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory property: Cornflower tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the liver, which can exacerbate jaundice symptoms and improve overall liver function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, supporting liver health.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried cornflower petals in an amount of one teaspoon for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat fresh water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a teapot or kettle.
Steep the dried cornflower petals in the hot water for a period of five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in lavender tea have been shown to effectively reduce bilirubin levels, a key component of jaundiced skin and eyes. Additionally, lavender's calming effects on the digestive system can help alleviate symptoms such as constipation, which is common in jaundiced patients. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, English lavender teas may provide relief from the discomforts associated with jaundice.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with jaundice, thereby alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort.

Laxative and carminative properties: The tea's laxative and carminative effects may help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation, a common symptom of jaundice, allowing the body to eliminate bilirubin more effectively.

Choleretic and hepatoprotective properties: Lavender's ability to stimulate bile production (choleretic effect) and protect liver cells from damage (hepatoprotective effect) may help alleviate symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and reduce inflammation in the liver.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of English lavender help to stimulate liver function and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, alleviating symptoms of jaundice.

Seeds: Containing potent antioxidants and flavonoids, the seeds of English lavender have been traditionally used to treat various liver disorders, including jaundice, by reducing inflammation and promoting overall hepatic health.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried lavender flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the lavender flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and transfer the tea liquid to a clean glass bottle with tight-fitting lid.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with jaundice because it has been shown to stimulate the liver's natural detoxification processes. Herbal teas such as turmeric, dandelion, and peppermint contain antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, alleviating yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, these teas can also help to improve digestion and reduce inflammation, further supporting the body's natural efforts to eliminate excess bilirubin.
remedy_properties: Milk Thistle (Silymarin): Contains silymarin, which helps to protect the liver from damage and regenerate damaged liver cells, thereby reducing bilirubin levels in the blood.
Dandelion Root: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce liver inflammation and promote the excretion of bile and bilirubin, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms.

Turmeric (Curcumin): Contains curcumin, a potent antioxidant that helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice, as well as stimulate bile production and improve liver function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Barks: Contain tannins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in the treatment of liver damage associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh tea leaves of camellia sinensis, ensuring 100% organic and pesticide-free production.
Steep one teaspoon of the harvested tea leaves in boiling water for precisely five minutes to release optimal flavonoids and antioxidants.
Allow the tea mixture to cool slightly before adding two pinches of dried peppermint leaf for enhanced digestive benefits.
Combine the cooled tea mixture with half a teaspoon of crushed ginger root, steeping for an additional three minutes to facilitate liver detoxification.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding solids and serving hot or chilled, ideally consuming twice daily for optimal jaundice relief.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal teas are liver and gallbladder-related conditions, such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, and bile duct obstruction. Herbs like turmeric, milk thistle, and dandelion root have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the liver and promote its natural detoxification process. Other herbs like peppermint and ginger can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and digestive discomfort often associated with these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaundice are natural remedies made from various herbs and plants that help alleviate symptoms and support the liver in its function to break down bilirubin, a pigment causing jaundice. Examples include dandelion tea, milk thistle tea, and turmeric tea, which help stimulate liver function and reduce inflammation. These teas can improve lives by reducing jaundice symptoms, promoting overall well-being, and boosting the body's natural healing processes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with jaundice is a blend of Dandelion root, Milk Thistle, and Turmeric. Dandelion root aids in liver detoxification and bile production, while Milk Thistle promotes liver cell regeneration and reduces oxidative stress. Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate liver inflammation and discomfort. This trio can be brewed together to create a soothing and healing tea that supports the liver's natural functions, promoting the body's natural recovery from jaundice. Drinking this tea regularly can also help prevent future occurrences.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy research : PTR, herbal teas from certain plants like Haplopappus baylahuen, Rosa moschata, and Peumus boldus have been found to have high antioxidant properties, specifically in scavenging ABTS(+) radicals and quenching pro-oxidant species such as hypochlorite and peroxynitrite. The study, which assessed the antioxidant properties of 13 herbs commonly consumed in southern regions, found that teas from these three plants showed a 5- to 7-fold higher activity than the least active herbs, with 150 mL of their tea being equivalent to around 200 mg of Trolox. This antioxidant potential may help protect the gastrointestinal tract against certain pathologically relevant oxidant species, suggesting that regular consumption of these herbal teas may be beneficial for conditions like jaundice, which is often caused by oxidative stress.
